title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App
text: MindBoost is a mental wellbeing app developed as part of the New Enterprise Development module at Dublin City University. As CEO of the project, I led a team of five students to design, research and pitch a digital solution for young adults struggling with stress and anxiety.

title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App
text: MindBoost is a mental wellbeing app developed as part of the New Enterprise Development module at Dublin City University. As CEO of the project, I led a team of five students to design, research and pitch a digital solution for young adults struggling with stress and anxiety.

title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App
text:
The Problem
Young people aged 18-30 face increasing levels of stress and anxiety, yet existing mental health solutions are often too clinical, expensive or time-consuming for daily use.

title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App
text:
Our Solution
MindBoost offers personalised daily mental wellbeing support through:

🧠 AI-personalised tips based on mood tracking
📓 Daily check-ins with journaling and breathing exercises
🎯 Short activities that fit into a 5-minute daily routine
👨‍⚕️ Direct therapist access for professional support




MindBoost App Mockup

title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App

title: MindBoost – Mental Wellbeing App
text:
Data Analysis


Code
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

insights = ['Daily check-ins\nhighly useful', 'Therapist\naccess', 'Willing to pay\n€4.99+/month']
values = [67.8, 67.6, 35.0]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
bars = ax.barh(insights, values, color=['#1B6B7B', '#29CADE', '#A0A4A8'])
ax.set_xlabel('Percentage (%)')
ax.set_xlim(0, 100)
for bar, val in zip(bars, values):
    ax.text(val + 1, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2, f'{val}%', va='center')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()





MindBoost User Research Results
